title: Layer registry row — one TSV row in layer_registry.tsv
description: Flat catalogue row, one per registered layer KIND. A layer is the unit of data the atlas resolves — pages consume layers; analyses produce layers; files contribute layers. The source_kind enum (file / analysis_result / operation / inline) decides how the librarian (resolve_layer.py) checks whether a layer is present. Many-to-many is supported: a layer_id may be produced by several analyses (cross-validate by joining analysis_registry.produces) and consumed by several analyses (analysis_registry.input_layer_types) and several hooks (hook_registry.requires_layers). Per the layer-abstraction design: 'modules produce layers; pages consume layers; APLR resolves layers'.

layer_id.description: Stable id. Convention: short snake_case noun (relatedness_res, mendelian_result, karyotype_calls, beagle_file, ...). Matches analysis_registry.produces / input_layer_types tokens.

source_kind.description: How the layer is materialized. file = a registered raw file (BEAGLE, sites, karyotype call sheet); analysis_result = output of an analysis row in analysis_registry; operation = computed on demand via an HTTP endpoint; inline = literal data embedded in a manifest.

entity_type.description: What kind of entity the layer rows describe (sample / pair / trio / variant_site / window / candidate / breakpoint / gene / interval / inline). Empty when not applicable.

description.description: One-line description of what the layer means biologically + structurally.

schema_path.description: Relative path to the layer-envelope or per-layer schema. Empty allowed while the contract is undefined.

example_path.description: Relative path to an example payload file. Empty allowed.

default_path.description: Relative path the librarian (resolve_layer.py) probes when source_kind=file. If the file exists at this path (relative to the registry root), the layer resolves to RESOLVED. Empty allowed; the librarian falls back to scope-driven lookup over the underlying set / input_values registries.

status.description: stub = the layer_id is registered (so it appears as KNOWN_MISSING, not UNKNOWN_CONTRACT) but no schema / no example yet.

definition_path.description: Optional path to a richer per-layer JSON when the TSV row isn't enough.

_tsv_empty_value: Empty cells are the zero-length string. The validator (check_layer_registry.py) treats empty as None for optional fields.
